_comment: Ground truth annotation — STROBE checklist for Gautret et al. 2020

article.title: Hydroxychloroquine and azithromycin as a treatment of COVID-19

study_type.notes: Open-label, non-randomized clinical trial. Authors call it a trial but no randomization, no blinding.

2.notes: Background on chloroquine efficacy against SARS-CoV-2

3.notes: Objective: evaluate effect of HCQ on viral load

4.notes: No formal study design description (cohort/case-control etc)

7.notes: Outcome: viral load at day 6. Exposure: HCQ +/- azithromycin

9.notes: No discussion of bias sources or efforts to address them

10.notes: No sample size calculation or justification

11.notes: No discussion of how quantitative variables were handled

12a.notes: Chi-square test, Fisher exact test mentioned

12c.notes: No discussion of missing data handling

13b.notes: 6 lost: 1 death, 3 ICU, 1 nausea, 1 left hospital

14a.notes: Table 1 with age, sex, clinical status per group

14b.notes: No reporting of missing data per variable

15.notes: Viral carriage percentages at day 6 per group

16a.notes: No adjusted or unadjusted estimates with CI

18.notes: Key results summarized: HCQ reduces viral load

20.notes: Interpretation given but one-sided, no caveats

22.notes: Funded by ANR, IHU Méditerranée Infection, PACA region
